name: Jelly Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the flour and butter until well mixed.
2. Add the eggs and granulated sugar; beat until well combined.
3. Cover the mixture and refrigerate overnight to allow the flavors to meld.
4. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
5. On a lightly floured surface, roll out the chilled dough until it is about 1/4-inch thick.
6. Using a round cookie cutter, cut out cookies and place them on an ungreased baking sheet.
7. Bake for 10-12 minutes or until very lightly browned. Allow to cool on a wire rack.
8. Once cooled, sandwich two cookies together and spread jelly between them.
9. Dust the cookies with powdered sugar on both sides.
10. The cookies taste better after a few days as the jelly absorbs into the cookie, making them softer.
Yield: 3 dozen sandwich cookies.
Tester's note: These sandwich cookies are quite tasty, especially when paired with a slightly tart jelly. This recipe is a cherished family heirloom from Karen Travis' grandmother on her father's side of the family, who immigrated from Dusseldorf, Germany to St. Louis.
